description:
Credibility gap: a mutation tester that reports no mutation score on its own code. `self_mutation_refused` (.spec/decisions/self_mutation_refused.md) makes in-process self-mutation impossible (the runner would crash mutating itself), so this needs a child-BEAM / external-process approach — essentially the test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs pattern aimed at mutagen_ex itself.

Produce a mutation score for the high-value modules (mutators/*, scope_resolver, cli, json_reporter) and publish it.

Larger effort than the other children — fine to land after the initial publish, but high-value for credibility.

Acceptance: a reproducible self-mutation score exists and is cited in the README/docs.

comments.text: ORCHESTRATOR NOTE: first implementer attempt was interrupted by a Claude spend/usage limit before it could commit or post IMPLEMENTED (infra, not a code defect). Its uncommitted scratch (a self_mutation_test.exs + self_mutation_harness.exs sketch) was saved for reference. Stage reset to the current epic HEAD (004f219, which now contains the .1/.2/.4 README sections) and a fresh --impl-only worker is being respawned on the remembers profile. This consumes attempts.impl (now 2 of 2).

IMPLEMENTED (commit 7f2478b on mutagen-bqd.3/dogfood-self-mutation)

Reproducible child-BEAM self-mutation score for mutagen_ex on its own high-value modules.

Mechanism (external-process, modeled on test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs):
- test/integration/support/self_mutation_harness.exs shadow-copies mutagen_ex's whole lib/ closure (minus lib/mix/tasks/) into a throwaway Mix project, mechanically rewriting the `MutagenEx`/`mutagen_ex` namespace to a `SelfMut`/`self_mut` shadow root so the self_mutation_refused guard (which only matches the literal `MutagenEx.` prefix) no longer fires. The shadow project wires the real mutagen_ex in as a path dep to supply `mix mutagen`, then the harness drives `mix mutagen --scope lib/self_mut/<m>.ex --tests <m>_test.exs` via System.cmd per target and parses the JSON report.
- The lowercase `mutagen_ex` rewrite must be global, not just the atom form: three cited tests read their own source from disk (e.g. File.read!("lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex")) for provenance assertions; the global rewrite keeps those reads pointed at the shadow tree so the baseline stays green.

Targets + result (byte-identical across two clean runs; content-addressed IDs + serial seeded execution):
- mutators: 1 site, 1 killed (thin registry module — expected)
- scope_resolver: 60 sites, 34 killed (0.57)
- json_reporter: 18 sites, 11 killed (0.61)
- AGGREGATE: 79 sites, 46 killed, kill rate 0.58

`cli` is intentionally excluded (documented in the harness): cli_test.exs is half Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/2 tests that bind to the excluded Mix task and the literal `MutagenEx.` guard prefix; two of them can never pass under the shadow rewrite (a `%SelfMut.Config{}` vs real `%MutagenEx.Config{}` expectation, and `SelfMut.MutationRunner` not being refused). Both redden the baseline before any mutation runs and neither exercises SelfMut.Cli. CLI parse logic is covered indirectly via scope_resolver, which consumes the scopes cli.ex parses.

Acceptance — reproducible self-mutation score cited in README: README "## Efficacy on real codebases" gains a "### Dogfood: mutagen_ex on its own source" subsection citing 46/79 (0.58), composing with the existing self-study/cross-project sections (not duplicating them).

Wiring: test tagged :self_mutation, excluded from default `mix test` (test/test_helper.exs), included in the `mix test.integration` alias (mix.exs). Run via `mix test --include self_mutation`.

Verification (Elixir 1.19.5):
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean
- mix test --seed 0: 640 tests, 0 failures (20 excluded) — default lane unaffected
- mix test test/integration/self_mutation_test.exs --include self_mutation --seed 0: 1 test, 0 failures (13.7s)

ORCHESTRATOR NOTE: the prior verifier exited having done its checks but its bw comment was malformed (posted literally '-F'); verdict body was lost. Respawning the verifier (no bounce consumed — posting failure, not a FAIL verdict). The malformed '-F' comment above is superseded by the next VERIFY comment.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 033814be-550f-48f5-ab4a-ac141d9e94a3
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/033814be-550f-48f5-ab4a-ac141d9e94a3.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 7 (all under test/** — yes). coverage_runner_test.exs, mutation_runner_test.exs, scope_resolver_property_test.exs, scope_resolver_test.exs, supervision_test.exs, test/support/cover_guard.exs (new), test/test_helper.exs. No lib/** or other product change.

Semantic checks:
- mix test --cover: test failures = 0 (3 of 4 full-suite runs), Enum.EmptyError crash present = no (0/4 runs), coverage printed = yes (78.96% Total), exit code = 3 (cause: threshold — tail reads "Coverage test failed, threshold not met: Coverage 78.96% / Threshold 90.00%"; stock Mix default, no test_coverage block in mix.exs).
- mix test (normal): 640 tests, 0 failures (19 excluded), exit 0.
- Base 196bb8c reproduced (read-only, main worktree): scope_resolver tests fail 2/2 under --cover on the two `refute Process.whereis(:cover_server)` assertions. EmptyError path also pre-existing per ticket. Fix removes both.
- Fix soundness (real resolution vs masking): SOUND.
  * The two named assertions are rewritten from bare `refute Process.whereis(:cover_server)` to before/after EQUALITY (`assert Process.whereis(:cover_server) == cover_server_before` and same for File.exists?("cover")). This is a strictly MORE correct statement of the real invariant — "the resolver does not start cover or create cover/" — and degrades to the original refute in normal mode (before == nil). Not a mask; it fixes a wrong assertion that conflated "harness owns cover" with "resolver started cover".
  * :cover_lifecycle exclusion is legitimate. Tagged tests manipulate the BEAM-wide :cover_server singleton (register sentinels, call :cover.stop, or drive CoverageRunner.run/1 which CORRECTLY refuses with :cover_already_running when cover is already registered). Under --cover the harness owns cover, so these cannot run as written — excluding ONLY under --cover is correct; they still run on every normal `mix test` (verified: the two key assertion tests execute, 2 tests/0 failures in normal mode; normal run excludes 19 vs --cover excludes 36, delta = the cover_lifecycle set). Not sweeping a product bug under the rug — CoverageRunner's refusal is the documented, desired behavior.
  * cover_guard.exs running_under_cover? uses a >1 module-count threshold so a test's own single-module cover session is not mistaken for the harness — sound. The mutation_runner on_exit guard skips the defensive :cover.stop only under harness cover, which is exactly the EmptyError source (stopping cover discarded harness instrumentation -> 0 modules -> reporter crash).

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only):
- One non-deterministic --cover failure observed in 1 of 4 full-suite runs: mutation_runner_test.exs:1181 ("r11 ... byte-identical before/after"), flagging a /tmp entry `mutagen_ex_selector_3587`. That entry originates from test_selector_test.exs:35 (System tmp dir, `mutagen_ex_selector_#{unique_integer}`), NOT from the runner under test. r11 snapshots /tmp and attributes any `mutagen_ex_`-prefixed entry to the runner, so a concurrent/lingering test_selector tmp dir inside the snapshot window is misattributed. This is PRE-EXISTING flaky cross-test /tmp contamination in the r11 assertion — the diff does not touch r11 (line 1181), DiskSnapshot, or test_selector_test; the only mutation_runner_test edits are the cover_guard require (line 33) and the on_exit guard (lines 55-72). In isolation r11 passes; 3 of 4 full runs were clean. Recommend a follow-up ticket to tighten r11's tmp attribution (e.g. snapshot only tmp entries created by THIS test's pid/window, or scope the prefix to the runner's own tmp namespace).

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 33781f87-780a-4c97-b7ae-fe26d3cec2b1
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/33781f87-780a-4c97-b7ae-fe26d3cec2b1.jsonl

CORRECTION: my immediately-preceding comment on this ticket (the "VERIFY: PASS ... 033814be ... coverage_runner / cover_guard" body) was POSTED IN ERROR — a stale /tmp file from a different ticket's verifier got published here due to a shell noclobber race. Disregard it entirely. THIS comment is the real verdict for mutagen-bqd.3.

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 5 (README.md, mix.exs, test/test_helper.exs, test/integration/self_mutation_test.exs, test/integration/support/self_mutation_harness.exs)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0
- No lib/ source touched (deliverable is docs + test wiring only; git diff 004f219..HEAD shows zero lib/ changes).

Semantic checks:
- Reproducibility (check A): RAN the harness directly via SelfMutationHarness.score/0 (not just the floor-asserting test). Cited number reproduces EXACTLY. Observed per-target: mutators 1/1 (1.0), scope_resolver 34/60 (0.5667), json_reporter 11/18 (0.6111). AGGREGATE 46/79, kill_rate 0.5823, 33 survivors. README cites "46 of 79 mutants killed, aggregate kill rate 0.58" and "33 survivors"; IMPLEMENTED comment cites scope_resolver 0.57, json_reporter 0.61, mutators 1/1 — all match. The committed test (self_mutation_test.exs) only pins a floor (kill_rate >= 0.5) plus structural invariants, NOT the exact 46/79; the exact figure is pinned only by README prose, which I confirmed empirically by running the harness. The floor is intentional (comment lines 57-66: set well below 0.58 to avoid brittleness on single-mutant drift). Acceptance ("a reproducible score exists and is cited") is met — it does reproduce. See Notes for the regression-pinning caveat.
- Scope reduction — cli (check B): JUSTIFIED. Read cli.ex (541 lines) and cli_test.exs (1068 lines). cli_test.exs has a `describe "Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/2 — dispatch table"` block (line 796+) that drives the REAL Mix task, which the shadow excludes from the copy (harness rejects lib/mix/tasks/; the real path-dep supplies `mix mutagen`). Two tests are structurally unpassable under the rewrite, exactly as documented: (1) line 858 asserts `--scope MutagenEx.MutationRunner` -> `:self_mutation_refused`, but the shadow rewrites it to `SelfMut.MutationRunner` which the real guard (mutation_runner.ex:162, prefix "Elixir.MutagenEx.") does NOT match; (2) line 809 dispatch test asserts a Config struct shape that diverges between %SelfMut.Config{} and the real Mix task's %MutagenEx.Config{}. Both redden the baseline before mutation and neither exercises Cli itself. Rationale holds; cli parse logic is covered indirectly via scope_resolver (which consumes the scopes cli.ex parses). Acceptance is met by the 3-of-4 score. Sound, documented gap — not an unjustified scope reduction.
- Mechanism soundness (check C): SOUND. Rewrite is a pure uniform token substitution (MutagenEx->SelfMut, mutagen_ex->self_mut) applied identically to lib + cited tests (harness rewrite/1, lines 255-259) — semantics-preserving; the guard's own prefix constant moves with everything so the shadow stays self-consistent, and the resolved scope (e.g. SelfMut.JsonReporter -> Elixir.SelfMut.JsonReporter) no longer matches the real guard's "Elixir.MutagenEx." prefix. Baseline IS green: all three targets ran with aborted=false and total>0, which the harness treats as proof the unmutated shadow tests passed before mutation. Real in-tree mutagen_ex is NOT mutated: the harness only File.read!s the real tree and writes exclusively into a uniquely-named subdir of System.tmp_dir!(); the real lib/ is untouched and the real self_mutation_refused guard is never bypassed in-tree.
- Default lane: 640 tests, 0 failures, 20 excluded at --seed 0. self_mutation test is tag-excluded (test_helper.exs adds :self_mutation to ExUnit exclude) and included only via --include self_mutation / the test.integration alias. No flaky failures observed.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. mix.exs (+1: self_mutation added to test.integration alias) and test_helper.exs (+self_mutation exclude tag) are correct, in-scope wiring.
- Project verification: PASS. mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean. mix test test/integration/self_mutation_test.exs --include self_mutation --seed 0 -> 1 test, 0 failures (17.3s).

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only — do NOT change the verdict):
- The exact 0.58 / 46-of-79 figure is reproducible by running the harness but is NOT pinned by a test assertion (only a >=0.5 floor is). Consider a follow-up that asserts the exact aggregate (or per-target totals) so README drift from the real score reddens CI rather than going silent. Acceptance is still met as written.
- cli was soundly excluded for genuine technical necessity (Mix-task-bound baseline tests incompatible with the shadow rewrite), documented in the harness; this is a tracked gap against the ticket's named 4-module list, not a defect.

title: Dogfood: child-BEAM self-mutation score
